skill.name: imagegen-frontend-web
skill.description: Elite frontend image-direction skill for generating premium, conversion-aware website design references. Generates one separate horizontal image per section with enforced composition variety, consistent palettes, and anti-generic design rules.
skill.summary: Generate premium, art-directed website design reference images with one horizontal image per section.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 95 pattern-based findings, but all are false positives. The SKILL.md file contains only markdown design instructions with no executable code, network calls, file system access, or cryptographic operations. The backtick characters flagged as shell execution are markdown formatting for numeric ranges. Terms like system, grid, and scan appear exclusively in frontend design contexts (design system, layout grid, visual scan order). No malicious intent detected.

content.value_statement: Standard AI image generation defaults to repetitive, generic website layouts with purple gradients and left-text/right-image heroes. This skill breaks those patterns by enforcing art-directed, premium frontend design references that developers can actually build from.
actual_capabilities: Generates one horizontal image per website section with consistent branding
Enforces varied composition anchors instead of default left-text/right-image layouts
Applies configurable design parameters for variance, density, and art direction
Provides themed site packs with predefined section structures
Includes anti-AI-slop rules to avoid generic gradients and fake KPIs
Outputs implementation-friendly references with clear hierarchy and spacing
limitations: Requires an image-capable model to render visual output
Does not output code or CSS directly
Design quality depends on the underlying image generation model
May need iteration for highly specific brand requirements

use_cases.title: Landing Page Concept Generation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams can quickly generate multi-section landing page visuals to present to stakeholders before development begins.

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Reference
use_cases.description: Developers receive structured, per-section design references that clearly communicate layout, spacing, typography scale, and component styling for accurate implementation.

use_cases.title: Portfolio and Agency Pitch Work
use_cases.description: Creative agencies can rapidly explore diverse visual directions for client pitches without relying on generic templates.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Landing Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a landing page for a sustainable fashion brand. Use a clean, editorial style with 6 sections.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: simple brief with default settings

prompt_templates.title: SaaS Product Site
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an 8-section marketing site for a fintech dashboard product. Style: premium SaaS with high implementation clarity, subtle gradients, and product-focused imagery.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: specifying industry and style constraints

prompt_templates.title: Creative Agency Portfolio
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a 12-section portfolio site for a motion design studio. Direction: bold creative statement, off-grid editorial layouts, giant statement hero, poster-like stacked storytelling, duotone image treatment.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: full creative direction with specific visual language

prompt_templates.title: E-commerce Product Page
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a 6-section product page for a premium audio equipment brand. Hero: mid editorial with strong product focus. Backgrounds: full-bleed product photo and soft radial vignette. CTAs must be unmistakable. Palette: deep charcoal, warm sand, single copper accent.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert: detailed per-section control with locked palette and composition

output_examples.input: Design a landing page for a sustainable fashion brand. Use a clean, editorial style with 6 sections.
output_examples.output: Section 1 of 6: Hero — Centered statement over full-bleed editorial photography, off-white background, sharp dark serif headline, thin underlined CTA. Section 2 of 6: Trust bar — Minimal logo strip on pure solid field. Section 3 of 6: Features — Editorial side-image at 50/50 split with asymmetric text pull. Section 4 of 6: Product showcase — Full-bleed image background with tonal overlay, bottom-left text cluster. Section 5 of 6: Testimonials — Stacked center quote with generous negative space. Section 6 of 6: CTA — Mini minimalist closing with tiny logo, short statement, thin primary pill.

output_examples.input: Create an 8-section marketing site for a fintech dashboard product.
output_examples.output: Section 1 of 8: Hero — Mid editorial hero with product UI panel stack on right, clear trust-driven headline on left, ink-to-graphite tonal gradient background. Section 2 of 8: Trust bar — Infinite brand marquee strip on neutral surface. Section 3 of 8: Features — Pristine gapless bento grid mixing large visual blocks with dense info panels. Section 4 of 8: Product showcase — Layered image crop frames showing dashboard screenshots. Section 5 of 8: Benefits — Alternating editorial blocks with varied composition anchors. Section 6 of 8: Testimonials — Split testimonial quote wall with strong typography contrast. Section 7 of 8: Pricing — Clean Swiss grid discipline with clear tier hierarchy. Section 8 of 8: CTA — Oversized headline plus tiny CTA hint on solid field.

best_practices: Provide a clear brief with industry, mood, and section count to get the strongest results
Use the themed keywords from the skill (minimalist, editorial, cinematic, SaaS) to trigger the correct design bias
Review all generated sections for consistency in palette and typography before finalizing
anti_patterns: Do not ask for code or CSS output from this skill; it generates visual references only
Do not request a single tall image containing the whole page; the skill enforces one image per section
Do not ignore the composition variety rule by accepting repeated left-text/right-image layouts

faq.question: What does this skill actually output?
faq.answer: This skill generates text descriptions and prompts for image-capable AI models to create horizontal website design reference images. It outputs one image per section, not code or CSS.

faq.question: Why does it generate one image per section instead of one full page?
faq.answer: Single full-page images become too dense and lose detail. Per-section images provide higher fidelity, clearer layout hierarchy, and easier implementation for developers.

faq.question: Can I use this with any AI model?
faq.answer: You need an image-generation-capable model. The skill works best with Claude or other multimodal models that can render images from detailed text prompts.

faq.question: How do I control the visual style?
faq.answer: Use keywords in your brief such as minimalist, editorial, cinematic, SaaS, agency, or e-commerce. The skill maps these to predefined design parameter sets.

faq.question: What is the default number of sections?
faq.answer: If you do not specify a section count, landing page defaults to 6 sections, full website or marketing site defaults to 8 sections, and portfolio defaults to 6 sections.

faq.question: Does this skill write HTML or CSS?
faq.answer: No. This skill is purely for visual direction and reference image generation. You can pair it with a coding skill to turn the generated references into actual code.
